items.note: climbing as chip stocks rebound ahead of a heavy week of Big Tech earnings

items.note: surging as Iran's ceasefire with the US collapses and Iran reportedly intercepts vessels in the Strait of Hormuz; Brent topped $90/bbl

items.name: Gold (per oz)

items.note: still digesting last week's Gemini 3.5 Pro delay report; reports Q2 earnings July 22

items.name: Oracle
items.note: bouncing modestly after last week's slide to a credit-rating cut near junk status over its AI data-center spending

items.name: Advanced Micro Devices
items.note: jumped in premarket trading after Microsoft said it will deploy AMD's Helios system across its data centers

items.name: Intuitive Surgical

items.name: ServiceNow

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: The SOX index jumped over 3% today, rebounding from Friday's bear-market close, helped by Microsoft's announcement that it will deploy AMD's new Helios system across its data centers — a concrete demand signal heading into a heavy week of earnings from Intel, Texas Instruments, and other bellwethers.

Cybersecurity names continue to outperform the rest of enterprise software, a divergence that traces back to IBM's recent commentary about clients shifting budget toward security and away from broader software spend.

weakest.reason: Tesla heads into Wednesday's earnings with Optimus production still at zero, and separately Unitree's profits reportedly halved — two data points that temper the 'robot boom' narrative even as Intuitive Surgical continues working off its own post-earnings guidance-driven drop.

Brent crude topped $90 a barrel as Iran's ceasefire with the US collapsed over the weekend and Iran reportedly intercepted vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, adding fresh inflation risk just as new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has signaled openness to a rate hike later this year.

outlook.nextStep: The chip sector's rebound and the AMD-Microsoft data center deal look like the more immediate, concrete signal worth a closer look heading into this week's earnings from Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM; the Iran-driven oil spike and the new Fed chair's rate-hike signaling are better watched than acted on until there's more clarity on where energy prices and policy land.

signals.title: Iran ceasefire collapses, Brent crude tops $90 as Hormuz tensions escalate
signals.body: Iran said its ceasefire with the US had effectively collapsed and reported intercepting vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, pushing Brent crude above $90/bbl and extending a roughly 30% surge from July lows. WTI has followed, last confirmed near the mid-$80s.
signals.watch: Whether shipping and insurance disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz spread, and whether the oil spike shows up in upcoming inflation data.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — this is a fast-moving geopolitical risk with wide potential outcomes in either direction.

signals.title: New Fed Chair Warsh leaves door open to a rate hike
signals.body: Under new Chair Kevin Warsh, the Fed held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% but shortened its policy statement, dropped forward guidance, and formed several task forces to review its process. Roughly half of FOMC members reportedly indicated they could support a rate hike later this year if inflation data doesn't cooperate — a notable shift in tone from prior guidance.
signals.watch: Upcoming inflation prints and any further public comments from Warsh or FOMC members on the hike odds.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a hawkish tone shift from a new Fed chair is a different kind of signal than routine data noise.

signals.title: Chip stocks rebound sharply as AMD-Microsoft data center deal lands
signals.body: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) rose more than 3% today after closing in bear-market territory Friday. AMD shares jumped in premarket trading after Microsoft confirmed it will use AMD's new Helios system in its data centers, offering a concrete demand data point just as Intel, Texas Instruments and other chip names prepare to report earnings this week.
signals.watch: Whether Intel and Texas Instruments' upcoming earnings confirm the sector-wide demand picture or reignite last week's capex-fatigue concerns.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a specific customer-demand announcement is a more concrete signal than the broader sentiment swings of the past week.

signals.title: Big Tech earnings week ahead, with Alphabet still facing Gemini delay questions
signals.body: Alphabet, Tesla, Intel and IBM are all due to report this week. Alphabet shares remain under pressure following last week's reports that its Gemini 3.5 Pro model is running behind schedule; its July 22 call will be watched closely for direct comment on the timeline.
signals.watch: Whether Alphabet addresses the Gemini delay directly and whether Big Tech capex commentary broadly supports or undercuts the AI infrastructure trade.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this week's earnings slate is likely to be the biggest near-term catalyst for the whole AI infrastructure group.

signals.title: Tesla heads into earnings with Optimus production still at zero
signals.body: Ahead of Wednesday's Q2 call, reporting notes Tesla's Optimus robot production count remains at zero, even as the stock trades lower today. Separately, Unitree's profits were reported to have halved, adding to signs that the 'humanoid robot boom' narrative is running ahead of near-term commercial reality, even as Intuitive Surgical's core medical-robotics business continues performing.
signals.watch: Any specific Optimus production timeline Tesla gives on Wednesday's call, and whether other humanoid-robotics names report similar margin pressure.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — the gap between the physical-AI narrative and near-term production numbers is worth watching closely before drawing conclusions.

signals.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows snap losing streak even as risk-off tone persists
signals.body: US spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled in $221.7 million, their largest daily inflow in two months, snapping a 10-day losing streak — even as broader crypto sentiment stays cautious with US-Iran hostilities now in their sixth day.
signals.watch: Whether the ETF inflow trend continues for a second day, which would suggest institutional demand is decoupling from the geopolitical risk-off mood.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a reversal in a 10-day outflow streak is a more concrete data point than day-to-day price moves.

signals.title: Cybersecurity keeps outperforming broader enterprise software
signals.body: Cybersecurity names continue to edge higher while most other enterprise software stocks trade mixed to lower, a divergence that traces back to IBM's recent warning that clients are shifting budget away from general software spend toward security and infrastructure.
signals.watch: Whether upcoming earnings from software names confirm the budget-reallocation story IBM described, or whether the divergence narrows.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on, but keep in mind some of this divergence may already be priced in after a week of the same trend.
